questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion's Marketplace?

options.text: To sell hardware accessories for Notion users
options.explanation: Marketplace is for templates, not physical products.

options.text: To showcase top Notion templates created by builders, makers, and creators
options.explanation: The article states Marketplace is where top creators showcase the possibilities of Notion.

options.text: To host community discussion forums
options.explanation: Discussion forums are hosted elsewhere, not in the Marketplace.

options.text: To provide technical support for Notion bugs
options.explanation: Technical support is handled through Notion’s support channels, not the Marketplace.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a criterion used to evaluate submissions for the Marketplace?

options.text: Value
options.explanation: Value is listed as a primary evaluation criterion.

options.text: Build quality
options.explanation: Build quality is explicitly mentioned in the article.

options.text: Popularity
options.explanation: Popularity (visits and duplications) is a listed factor.

options.text: Color scheme
options.explanation: Color scheme is not part of the evaluation criteria; visual design is, but not a specific color scheme.

questions.question: How often are featured templates and creators refreshed in the Marketplace?

options.text: Every day
options.explanation: The article mentions weekly to monthly intervals, not daily.

options.text: Weekly to monthly, depending on language
options.explanation: Featured templates and creators are refreshed at varying timeframes, from weekly to monthly.

options.text: Once a year
options.explanation: Refreshes happen more frequently than annually.

options.text: Only when a new template is submitted
options.explanation: Refreshes follow a schedule, not solely on new submissions.

questions.question: When assessing the "Novelty" criterion, which aspect is specifically considered?

options.text: The price of the template relative to competitors
options.explanation: Price is evaluated under the "Price" criterion, not Novelty.

options.text: Whether the template uses new Notion features or showcases integrations in a fresh way
options.explanation: Novelty looks at new use‑cases, new features, and unique integrations.

options.text: The number of pages the template contains
options.explanation: Page count is not a factor for Novelty.

options.text: The popularity measured by visits and duplications
options.explanation: Popularity is a separate criterion.

questions.question: Under the "Build quality" criterion, which of the following is emphasized?

options.text: Inclusion of advanced Notion features such as databases, buttons, AI, and embeds
options.explanation: Build quality rewards templates that leverage Notion’s powerful features.

options.text: Having the smallest possible file size for faster loading
options.explanation: File size is not mentioned as a factor for Build quality.

options.text: Using only plain text and simple tables
options.explanation: Templates that rely only on text and tables are considered lower quality.

options.text: Pricing the template at a premium to signal exclusivity
options.explanation: Pricing falls under the "Price" criterion, not Build quality.

questions.question: Which factor would most likely increase a template's chance of being featured due to the "Seasonality" criterion?

options.text: A holiday budgeting template released in December
options.explanation: Seasonality favors templates relevant to the time of year, such as holiday budgeting in December.

options.text: A generic note‑taking template available year‑round
options.explanation: Generic templates lack seasonal relevance.

options.text: A template about ancient Roman history
options.explanation: Historical content is not tied to a current season.

options.text: A template without a thumbnail image
options.explanation: Thumbnail quality is evaluated separately from Seasonality.

options.text: The visual quality and relevance of the homepage thumbnail image
options.explanation: Thumbnail evaluation focuses on how well the image showcases the template.

options.text: The number of images included inside the template
options.explanation: Quantity of images is not the focus; it's the thumbnail’s presentation.

options.text: The file size of the template for faster download
options.explanation: File size is unrelated to the thumbnail criterion.

options.text: The price displayed on the thumbnail
options.explanation: Pricing is covered under the "Price" criterion.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a way Notion promotes featured templates?

options.text: Featured templates displayed on the Marketplace home page
options.explanation: This is a core promotion method listed in the article.

options.text: Featured collections that group templates by use‑case
options.explanation: Featured collections are explicitly mentioned.

options.text: Paid advertising on external websites
options.explanation: The article does not mention paid external advertising as a promotion channel.

options.text: Social media posts highlighting featured templates
options.explanation: Social posts are part of the promotion strategy.

questions.question: A template is free, has high popularity, but lacks advanced Notion features and serves a very generic purpose. Which criteria are most likely to hinder its selection for featured status?

options.text: Value and Build quality
options.explanation: Value suffers because the purpose is generic, and Build quality suffers due to missing advanced features.

options.text: Popularity and Price
options.explanation: Popularity is strong, and the template is free, so these are not hindering factors.

options.text: Novelty and Seasonality
options.explanation: Novelty and Seasonality are not mentioned as problems for this scenario.

options.text: Visual design and Thumbnail
options.explanation: While important, the question highlights value and build quality as the main issues.

questions.question: If a creator submits a template that utilizes a newly released Notion integration and aligns with an upcoming major news theme, which combination of criteria does this most strongly satisfy?

options.text: Timing and Novelty
options.explanation: The template matches a current news trend (Timing) and uses a new integration (Novelty).

options.text: Price and Popularity
options.explanation: Price and popularity are not highlighted in this scenario.

options.text: Visual design and Thumbnail
options.explanation: These aspects are not directly addressed by the described attributes.

options.text: Seasonality and Value
options.explanation: Seasonality relates to time of year, not news themes, and Value is not the focus here.
